content.body: I am there during the first hour after all, so I can do it

Richard Gibson: I definitely do not think it is worth including info blocks with spec text in tests and intend to continue not doing so; as a consumer I always found them annoying even when they weren't out of date (which was rarely)
content.formatted_body: Richard Gibson: I definitely do not think it is worth including info blocks with spec text in tests and intend to continue not doing so; as a consumer I always found them annoying even when they weren't out of date (which was rarely)

content.body: and as a test author they're annoying to add and this discourages having good coverage

I am sympathetic, but https://github.com/tc39/test262/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#test-case-style documents that "_`info` should contain relevant, direct quotes from ECMAScript if possible_", further backed up by https://github.com/tc39/test262/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#info . And as a test reviewer, the context can be important. Seems like a good topic for the next maintainers meeting... I wonder if we can find guidance that's closer to the Pareto frontier.
content.formatted_body: I am sympathetic, but https://github.com/tc39/test262/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#test-case-style documents that "info should contain relevant, direct quotes from ECMAScript if possible", further backed up by https://github.com/tc39/test262/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#info . And as a test reviewer, the context can be important. Seems like a good topic for the next maintainers meeting... I wonder if we can find guidance that's closer to the Pareto frontier.

content.body: I would love to change this requirement, the time that the info blocks have saved me as a reviewer, is minuscule compared to the time that they have cost me as a writer

$ rg 'info:' | wc -l
   38544
$ rg 'description:' | wc -l
   53572

looks like 28% of tests are already ignoring that (probably disproportionately recent ones)
I vote we just change it from "should" to "may" and go about our lives
i'd expect the % to be higher for non-staging tests

$ rg 'info:' test | wc -l
   36580
$ rg 'description:' test | wc -l
   53426
I would go one step further and say you should move existing info blocks because they're more than likely wrong/outdated
